zh.lifeSummary: 當Richard Andrews於1404年出生在英國諾薩普頓史爾吉爾斯比時，他的父親Sir, John Andrews , I31歲，母親Joan Welles22歲。他於1434年與Catherine Burbeck在英國英格蘭諾薩普頓史爾結婚。他們至少是4個兒子和1個女兒的父母。他去世於1450年在他的家鄉享年46歲。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Richard Andrews于1404年出生在英国諾薩普頓史爾吉爾斯比时，他的父亲Sir, John Andrews , I31岁，母亲Joan Welles22岁。他于1434年与Catherine Burbeck在英国英格兰諾薩普頓史爾结婚。他们至少是4个儿子和1个女儿的父母。他去世于1450年在他的家乡享年46岁。

nameMeanings.name: Andrews
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English: variant of Andrew , with genitival or post-medieval excrescent -s. This is the usual southern English patronymic form, also found in Wales; the Scottish and northern English form is Anderson . In North America, this surname has absorbed various cognates from other languages, e.g. Polish Andrzejewski , Slovenian Andrejčič, Serbian and Croatian Andrić (see Andric ), and Czech Ondráček (see Ondracek ).
Irish and Scottish: Anglicized form of Scottish Gaelic Mac Aindreis or Irish Mac Aindriú, see McAndrew .

History: This was a common name among the early settlers in New England. Robert Andrews emigrated in 1635 from Norwich, England, to Ipswich, MA. Even before 1635, one Thomas Andrews is recorded as being established in Hingham. A certain William Andrews was a member of John Davenport's company, which sailed from Boston in 1638 to found the New Haven colony.

variants.name: Andrus
meanings: History: William Andrus came to Boston in 1635 and moved to New Haven in 1639, where he died in 1676.

variants.name: Andrew
meanings: English and Scottish: from the usual vernacular English form (recorded from the 13th century onward) of the New Testament Greek personal name Andreas . In North America, the English form of the surname has absorbed many cognates from other languages, e.g. Czech Ondřej (see Ondrey ) and Albanian Ndreu , and also their patronymics and other derivatives (see examples at Andrews ).
History: The surname Andrew was first brought to North America from England by Robert Andrew (died 1668), who settled in Boxford, MA.

variants.name: Andros
meanings: English: variant of   Andrews  . This surname is very rare in Britain.

American shortened form of Greek Andronikos (see Andron 2) or of any other surname based on a personal name beginning with Andr- (from andros, genitive of anēr ‘man, male’), e.g. Androutsos, including their patronymics and other derivatives, such as Andronikidis.

History: William Andros came to VA in 1617 and died there c. 1655. Sir Edmund Andros (1637–1714) was the British colonial governor of several provinces in America between 1674 and 1698, most notably NY (1674–81).

nameMeanings.name: Richard
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: One of the most enduringly successful of the Old French personal names introduced into Britain by the Normans. It is of Germanic (Frankish) origin, derived from rīc ‘power’ + hard ‘strong, hardy’. It has enjoyed continuous popularity in England from the Conquest to the present day, influenced by the fact that it was borne by three kings of England, in particular Richard I ( 1157–99 ). He was king for only ten years ( 1189–99 ), most of which he spent in warfare abroad, taking part in the Third Crusade and costing the people of England considerable sums in taxes. Nevertheless, he achieved the status of a folk hero, and was never in England long enough to disappoint popular faith in his goodness and justice. He was also Duke of Aquitaine and Normandy and Count of Anjou, fiefs which he held at a time of maximum English expansion in France. His exploits as a leader of the Third Crusade earned him the nickname ‘Coeur de Lion’ or ‘Lionheart’ and a permanent place in popular imagination, in which he was even more firmly enshrined by Sir Walter Scott's novel Ivanhoe ( 1820 ).

variants.name: Rikert
meanings: One of the most enduringly successful of the Old French personal names introduced into Britain by the Normans. It is of Germanic (Frankish) origin, derived from rīc ‘power’ + hard ‘strong, hardy’. It has enjoyed continuous popularity in England from the Conquest to the present day, influenced by the fact that it was borne by three kings of England, in particular Richard I ( 1157–99 ). en.lifeSummary: When Richard Andrews was born in 1404, in Kilsby, Northamptonshire, England, his father, Sir, John Andrews , I, was 31 and his mother, Joan Welles, was 22. He married Catherine Burbeck in 1434, in Northamptonshire, England, United Kingdom. They were the parents of at least 4 sons and 1 daughter. He died in 1450, in his hometown, at the age of 46.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Richard Andrews est né en 1404, à Kilsby, Northamptonshire, Angleterre, son père, Sir, John Andrews , I, avait 31 ans et sa mère, Joan Welles, avait 22 ans. Il a épousé Catherine Burbeck en 1434, à Northamptonshire, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni. Ils ont eu au moins 4 fils et 1 fille. Il est décédé en 1450, dans sa ville natale, à l’âge de 46 ans.

de.lifeSummary: Als Richard Andrews 1404 in Kilsby, Northamptonshire, England geboren wurde, war sein Vater Sir, John Andrews , I 31 und seine Mutter Joan Welles 22. Er hat 1434 in Northamptonshire, England, United Kingdom Catherine Burbeck geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 4 Söhne und 1 Tochter. Er starb 1450 im Alter von 46 Jahren in seinem Geburtsort.

it.lifeSummary: Quando Richard Andrews è nato il nel 1404 nel Kilsby, Northamptonshire, England, il suo padre, Sir, John Andrews , I, aveva 31 anni e il suo madre, Joan Welles, aveva 22 anni. Lui sposato Catherine Burbeck nel 1434, nel Northamptonshire, Inghilterra, Regno Unito. Erano genitori di almeno 4 figli e 1 figlia. Morì nel 1450, nel sua cittadina, all'età di 46 anni.

ko.lifeSummary: Richard Andrews(이)가 1404년 영국 노르섬프토실르 킬스비에서 태어났을 때, 그의 아버지 Sir, John Andrews , I(은)는 31세였고, 그의 어머니 Joan Welles(은)는 22세였다. 그는 1434년 영국 잉글랜드 노르섬프토실르에서 Catherine Burbeck(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 네 명의 아들과 한 명의 딸을 두었다. 그는 1450년 그의 고향에서 46세의 나이로 사망했다.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Richard Andrews родился в 1404 г. в местности килсби, нортамптоншир, Англия, его отцу по имени Sir, John Andrews , I было 31 год, а матери, которую звали Joan Welles, 22. Он женился на женщине по имени Catherine Burbeck в 1434 г. в местности нортамптоншир, Англия, Великобритания. У них было по крайней мере 4 сына и 1 дочь. Он умер в 1450 г. в его родном городе в возрасте 46 лет.
